title: Namibia Premier League

infobox.value: [NFA Cup](/wiki/NFA_Cup "NFA Cup")   [Namibian Newspaper Cup](/wiki/Namibian_Newspaper_Cup "Namibian Newspaper Cup")  [Standard Bank Super Cup](/wiki/Namibian_Super_Cup "Namibian Super Cup")[[1]](#cite_note-1)
[Champions League](/wiki/CAF_Champions_League "CAF Champions League")   [Confederation Cup](/wiki/CAF_Confederation_Cup "CAF Confederation Cup")
[Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") [Jerome Louis](/wiki/Jerome_Louis "Jerome Louis") [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") Richard Kavendji | Black Africa  Hotspurs | 12

|  |  | | --- | --- | | Men | * [National team](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team "Namibia national football team") ([Results](/wiki/Namibia_national_football_team_results "Namibia national football team results")) * [Olympic (U-23)](/w/index.php?title=Namibia_national_under-23_football_team&action=edit&redlink=1 "Namibia national under-23 football team (page does not exist)") * [U-20](/wiki/Namibia_national_under-20_football_team "Namibia national under-20 football team") * [U-17](/wiki/Namibia_national_under-17_football_team "Namibia national under-17 football team") | | Women | * [National team](/wiki/Na

(**NPL**) was the highest level of domestic [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") until it was disbanded following the 2018-19 season. The league was established in 1990 and was trimmed to 12 teams from the traditional 16 in 2005. It was disbanded in 2020 after ongoing problems with the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association"), which finally ended their relationship. The NFA founded the [Namibia Football Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Premier_League "Namibia Football Premier League") to replace it. The new league kicked off with the 2022-2023 season with African Stars against UNAM FC at the [Hage Geingob Rugby Stadium](/wiki/Hage_Geingob_Rugby_Stadium "Hage Geingob Rugby Stadium").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-homeless-3)

["Namibia Super Cup gets new sponsor | The Soccer Pages"](https://web.archive.org/web/20140415043239/http://www.thesoccerpages.com/news/206/namibia-super-cup-gets-new-sponsor). Archived from [the original](http://www.thesoccerpages.com/news/206/namibia-super-cup-gets-new-sponsor) on 2014-04-15.

["*Namibia football starts new chapter.* Southern Times, 11 December 2020"](https://web.archive.org/web/20201213132747/https://southerntimesafrica.com/site/news/namibia-football-starts-new-chapter). Archived from [the original](https://southerntimesafrica.com/site/news/namibia-football-starts-new-chapter) on 13 December 2020. Retrieved 15 January 2021.
[*"Homeless" NPL refuses to yield.* The Namibian, 22 December 2020.](https://www.namibian.com.na/97549/read/Homeless-NPL-refuses-to-yield)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Not to be confused with [Namibia Premiership](/wiki/Namibia_Premiership "Namibia Premiership").

Football league

Namibia Premier League

The **Namibia Premier League** (**NPL**) was the highest level of domestic [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") in [Namibia](/wiki/Namibia "Namibia") until it was disbanded following the 2018-19 season. The league was established in 1990 and was trimmed to 12 teams from the traditional 16 in 2005. It was disbanded in 2020 after ongoing problems with the [Namibia Football Association](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Association "Namibia Football Association"), which finally ended their relationship. The NFA founded the [Namibia Football Premier League](/wiki/Namibia_Football_Premier_League "Namibia Football Premier League") to replace it. The new league kicked off with the 2022-2023 season with African Stars against UNAM FC at the [Hage Geingob Rugby Stadium](/wiki/Hage_Geingob_Rugby_Stadium "Hage Geingob Rugby Stadium").[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-homeless-3)

* 1992 : [Ramblers](/wiki/Ramblers_F.C. "Ramblers F.C.") ([Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek"))

* 2002–03 : [Chief Santos](/wiki/Chief_Santos "Chief Santos") ([Tsumeb](/wiki/Tsumeb "Tsumeb"))

* 2007–08 : [Orlando Pirates](/wiki/Orlando_Pirates_S.C. "Orlando Pirates S.C.") ([Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek"))
* 2008–09 : [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") ([Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek"))
* 2009–10 : [African Stars](/wiki/African_Stars_F.C. "African Stars F.C.") ([Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek"))
* 2010–11 : [Black Africa](/wiki/Black_Africa_S.C. "Black Africa S.C.") ([Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek"))

* [2015–16](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_Namibia_Premier_League "2015–16 Namibia Premier League"): [Tigers](/wiki/United_Africa_Tigers "United Africa Tigers") ([Windhoek](/wiki/Windhoek "Windhoek"))
